mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) are a leading indicator of consumer spending, the main driver of U.S. GDP. A month-over-month increase in RSAFS signals accelerating consumption, which lifts corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS level and 20-day forward return is less than 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS m/m changes are followed by negative returns in more than half of occurrences over a 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds a high threshold like 8%, it indicates severe labor market slack. Such extreme readings historically trigger aggressive fiscal and monetary stimulus, and mean-reversion in the labor market leads to improved economic conditions, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 8%, the subsequent 60-day return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the average excess return compared to low-unemployment periods is not statistically positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with an upturn in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that accommodative monetary policy is successfully stimulating the real economy. This joint signal of policy effectiveness and economic rebound drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and INDPRO rising, the 60-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and stable (sub-215 index level and month-on-month change below 0.2%), discount rates compress and equity risk premia shrink, supporting higher stock valuations. This mechanism is active only during lowflation regimes and predicts positive forward returns.
failure_condition: When lowflation conditions hold, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative more often than not, or the mean return is less than 0.5% over a rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) is a coincident indicator of economic contraction. A declining PAYEMS level directly signals lower corporate earnings and deteriorating consumer income, leading to negative short-term equity returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS level and 5-day forward return is positive over any 1-year window, or the average 5-day return following PAYEMS declines is greater than 0% (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling to extremely low levels (below 600,000 units) indicate an oversold housing market that historically precedes a rebound. This contrarian signal suggests the worst of the housing downturn is over, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After HOUST falls below 600,000, the average 60-day SPX return is negative, or the frequency of positive returns is less than 55% over multiple occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously contracting GDP (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter drop) and very high unemployment (UNRATE above 7%) signal a severe recession. This combination historically triggers powerful fiscal and monetary responses and sets the stage for a recovery rally, producing positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 60-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS at or below 0.25%), conventional monetary policy is exhausted. The commitment to keep rates near zero and the absence of tightening risk support asset prices by compressing discount rates and encouraging risk-taking, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is <= 0.25%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is less than 0.2% over a 2-year rolling window.
